paper.title: ImageWAM: Do World Action Models Really Need Video Generation, or Just Image Editing?

claims.claim: Video-based WAMs face three coupled limitations: dense multi-frame future tokens make inference costly, full video prediction spends capacity on action-irrelevant details, and long-horizon future imagination may introduce errors that mislead action prediction.

locations.span: However, this bridge also reveals a mismatch...
However, video-based WAMs face three coupled limitations...

evidence.description: Efficiency comparison (Table 5): FastWAM-IDM (full video) 63.65 TFLOPs, 1081ms vs ImageWAM 9.72 TFLOPs, 263ms
Future-video artifact analysis showing distorted observations around task-relevant objects (Figure 5)

claims.claim: Image editing models offer a more direct visual generative prior for language-conditioned manipulation: they provide instruction-to-change alignment, an easier goal/change proxy, and compact inference.

locations.span: We argue that image editing models offer a more direct visual generative prior...
We study image editing from a robotics perspective...

evidence.description: Three properties identified: instruction-to-change alignment, easier goal/change proxy, compact inference

claims.claim: ImageWAM repurposes pretrained image editing models for robot action prediction by extracting KV caches from editing denoising as compact world-action context, without decoding dense multi-frame videos.

locations.span: The editing cache is defined as the set of layer-wise KV pairs...
At inference time, ImageWAM avoids full future-video generation...

evidence.description: C_edit^τ = {(K_ℓ^τ, V_ℓ^τ)}^L_{ℓ=1} = f_edit^τ(o_t, l). At inference: single editing forward step yields cache, then action denoising.

claims.claim: ImageWAM outperforms standard VLA baselines without additional policy pretraining across simulator and real-world experiments.

evidence.description: RoboTwin 2.0: ImageWAM 93.38% vs π₀ 62.16%, vs π₀.₅ 79.75%, vs ABot-M0 80.80% (all without P.T.)
Real-world: ImageWAM 84.5% vs π₀ 55.8% (+28.7 pts), vs π₀.₅ 72.3% (+12.2 pts)
LIBERO: ImageWAM 98.4% vs OpenVLA 76.5%, vs GR00T N1 76.5%

claims.claim: ImageWAM matches competitive video-generation-based WAMs without additional policy pretraining.

locations.span: Compared with video-generation-based WAMs, ImageWAM reaches comparable performance...

evidence.description: RoboTwin 2.0: ImageWAM 93.38% vs FastWAM 91.83%, vs Motus 87.80%, vs LingBot-VA 92.20%
LIBERO: ImageWAM 98.4% vs FastWAM 97.6%, vs Motus 97.7%, vs LingBot-VA 98.5%

claims.claim: ImageWAM reduces FLOPs to 1/6 and latency to 1/4 of video-based WAMs.

locations.span: It also reduces FLOPs to 1/6 and latency to 1/4 of video-based WAMs.

evidence.description: ImageWAM: 9.72 TFLOPs, 263ms vs FastWAM-IDM: 63.65 TFLOPs, 1081ms. Ratio: 6.55× FLOPs, 4.1× latency

claims.claim: ImageWAM achieves strong real-world performance on diverse tasks including long-horizon manipulation, deformable-object manipulation, visual occlusion, and fine-grained control.

locations.span: ImageWAM achieves an average success rate of 84.5%...

evidence.description: T1 (Stack Bowls): 94%, T2 (Fold Towel): 84%, T3 (Drawer & Marker): 78%, T4 (Hang Cup): 82%

claims.claim: Editing caches focus on task-relevant change regions, as shown by attention analysis.

locations.span: Attention analysis further shows that editing caches focus on task-relevant change regions...

evidence.description: Attention visualization (Figure 4) shows editing cache attention maps concentrating on regions where task-relevant visual change occurs

claims.claim: The gains from ImageWAM are not merely due to stronger image recognition or language alignment, but from editing-specific pretraining.

evidence.description: ImageWAM (LIBERO): 98.4% vs BagelVLA w/ K.F.: reported lower performance; ImageWAM (RoboTwin Clean): 84.4% vs BagelVLA w/ K.F.: 75.3%

claims.claim: Instruction conditioning and editing-oriented feature extraction are important for obtaining action-relevant representations.

locations.span: Our analyses further show that instruction conditioning and editing-oriented feature extraction are important...

evidence.description: Comparison with BagelVLA (unified model) shows editing-specific pretraining provides significant advantage

claims.claim: ImageWAM shows strong robustness to visual domain shifts, dramatically outperforming video-based WAM on LIBERO-Plus.

locations.span: Under the LIBERO-Plus setting, ImageWAM maintains an average success rate of 83.1%

evidence.description: LIBERO-Plus: ImageWAM (FLUX.2 4B) 83.1% vs FastWAM 51.5%. Camera: 80.8% vs 16.4%, Layout: 80.5% vs 60.7%

claims.claim: Scaling the image editing backbone from 4B to 9B parameters improves performance on LIBERO-Plus.

locations.span: Effect of using a larger editing backbone on LIBERO-Plus

evidence.description: FLUX.2 4B: 83.1% → FLUX.2 9B: 85.2% (+2.1 pts). Largest gains on Robot (+8.4 pts), Language (+3.8 pts), Layout (+2.6 pts)
